info.title: BGeometrics Bitcoin API On-Chain
info.description:
Styles: OpenApi  ReDoc  Scalar

Subscriptions

Unlock higher request limits and access to additional datasets with a subscription.
👉Subscription BGeometrics

Usage Guidelines
  • Example: https://bitcoin-data.com/v1/sopr
  • The access token can be sent as a URL parameter (?token=...) or in the header: Authorization: Bearer <token>
  • Example with the token: https://bitcoin-data.com/v1/cdd?token=XXX
  • Date format: YYYY-MM-DD (applies to day, startday, endday) https://bitcoin-data.com/v1/mvrv?startday=2025-01-01&endday=2025-01-07
  • Pagination: use the 'page' and 'size' parameters to paginate.
  • Free plan: up to 8 requests per hour and 15 requests per day.
  • Subscription agents AI: https://bitcoin-data.com/bguser/ai-agent-subscription.html

tags.name: ETF BTC
tags.description: The daily flows and aggregates of Bitcoin of the ETF, Blackrock (IBIT), The Grayscale Bitcoin Trust (GBTC), Fidelity (FBTC), Ark Invest /21Shares (ARKB), Bitwise (BITB), Franklin (EZBC), Investco / Galaxy (BTCO), Vaneck (HODL), Valkyrie (BRR) and Wisdomtree (BTCW).

tags.name: Derivatives
tags.description: Derivates Basis. The basis is the difference between the price of futures contracts and the spot market price of Bitcoin. Basis > 0 (contango), the futures price is higher than the spot price. This indicates market optimism (traders expect BTC to rise). Basis < 0 (backwardation), the futures price is below the spot price. This indicates pessimism or high selling pressure in derivatives. Funding Rate. It is an interest rate that is periodically paid between traders of perpetual futures contracts to balance out price differences between futures contracts and the spot market. Futures Global 1h. Measures the proportion of accounts (traders) that hold long positions versus short positions in Bitcoin futures. Updated hourly. Open Interes Futures. Bitcoin futures and options contracts in circulation on the market. Open Interes 1h. Total number of open futures or perpetual contracts that have not yet been closed or settled at that time on BTC. Updated hourly. Top Trader Position 1h. Top Traders Long/Short Positions for Bitcoin, they refer to how the largest accounts (“top traders”) are positioned in the futures market. Updated hourly. Top Trader Long Account 1h. How the largest accounts (the “top traders”) are positioned in the BTC futures market. Updated hourly. Taker Buy Sell Volume 1h. Who immediately executes the order in the Bitcoin order book. Updated hourly.

tags.name: Cryptos
tags.description:
Crypto: Cosmos (ATOM), Ethereum Classic (ETC), XDC Network (XDC), Hedera (HBAR), NEAR Protocol (NEAR), VeChain (VET), Tezos (XTZ), NEM (XEM), Internet Computer (ICP), Stacks (STX), Injective (INJ). 

Crypto2: Stellar (XLM), Litecoin (LTC), Polkadot (DOT), Monero (XMR), Uniswap (UNI), Bitcoin Cash (BCH), Shiba Inu (SHIB), Algorand (ALGO), Filecoin (FIL). 

Crypto3: Cosmos (ATOM), Ethereum Classic (ETC), XDC Network (XDC), Hedera (HBAR), NEAR Protocol (NEAR), VeChain (VET), Tezos (XTZ), NEM (XEM), Internet Computer (ICP), Stacks (STX), Injective (INJ).

tags.name: Difficulty Btc
tags.description: Difficulty: how difficult it is to find a hash below the target compared to the minimum difficulty of the system.

tags.name: Realized Price
tags.description: Bitcoin Realized Price is the average price at which bitcoins were bought/spent/transferred.

tags.name: Hashrate
tags.description: ### Available metrics: Hashrate is the amount of computing power being used on the network to mine and validate blocks. Hashribbons is a market indicator that uses Bitcoin's hash rate data to identify potential buying opportunities during miner capitulation periods. HashPrice is a metric that measures how much money a miner earns for each TH/s (terahash per second) of mining power per day.

tags.name: BTC Terminal Price
tags.description: Export BTC Terminal Price data as CSV.

tags.name: Addresses Coins
tags.description: Bitcoin Coins by Address (or Coins by Address Balance) refers to the total amount of BTC held in addresses whose balance falls within a certain range of BTC.

tags.name: Hodl Waves
tags.description: ### Available metrics: HODL Waves are the percentage of bitcoins based on how long ago they were purchased. HODL Waves Supply is an on-chain metric that analyzes the length of time bitcoins have remained unmoved, offering insights into the accumulation and distribution habits of Bitcoin holders.

tags.name: NRPL
tags.description: Bitcoin NRPL (Net Realized Profit Loss). It measures BTC of profit or loss realized by bitcoin holders over last day. The NRPL indicator compares the selling price of coins to their acquisition price (based on the last time they were moved).

tags.name: Realized Cap
tags.description: Bitcoin Realized Capitalization

tags.name: M2
tags.description: It is necesary subscription to get the data of M2. Please contact us to get access to this data. M2 Bank It is the total amount of liquid and semi-liquid money in an economy. It includes cash in circulation and demand deposits. M2 Global Global liquidity or M2 money supply is a measure of the total amount of money available in an economy. M2 YoY Change Growth in annual change of M2

tags.name: BTC Technical Indicators
tags.description: Bitcoin Technical Indicators: rsi, macd, macdsignal, macdhist, sma7, sma50, sma200, ema7, ema50, ema200

tags.name: Stablecoin Supply
tags.description: The circulation of these stablecoins, usdt, usdc, dai, busd, gusd and pax.

tags.name: S2F
tags.description: Stock to Flow deviation

tags.name: Exchanges
tags.description: Exchange Reserve Trend (30d), the percentage change in the total BTC held on exchanges over the last 30 days. A negative value indicates that BTC is leaving exchanges (withdrawals exceed deposits), which is generally interpreted as a bullish signal — investors are moving coins to self-custody, reducing available selling supply. A positive value indicates that BTC is accumulating on exchanges (deposits exceed withdrawals), which is generally interpreted as a bearish signal — investors are preparing to sell. This metric helps identify sustained directional shifts in exchange reserves beyond daily noise.

tags.name: Coin Days Destroyed
tags.description: ### Available metrics: Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) is the sum of the product of the number of coins spent and the number of days those coins were inactive before being spent, calculated for all coins spent on a given day. Supply Adjusted Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) is CDD value adjusted by supply. It is calculated as the value of CDD divided by total supply. Binary Coin Days Destroyed is binary value and if Supply Adjusted Coin Days Destroyed (Supply-Adjusted CDD) is larger than average Supply-Adjusted CDD, Binary Coin Days points to '1' and points to '0' if not. Supply Adjusted Dormancy is the average age of all coins spent on a given day, adjusted by supply. It is calculated as the value of Dormancy divided by total supply. Average Dormancy is the average age of all coins spent on a given day. It is calculated as the total Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) divided by the total volume of coins spent on that day. VDD Value Destroyed Days combines Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) with the price to measure how much 'old economic value' is moving on the network. VDD = (BTC × age in days) x USD Price. VDD Multiple (Value Days Destroyed Multiple) then divides a 30-day average of VDD by a 365 average to compare near-term spending velocity with a yearly average of spending velocity. MVOCDD Median Value of Coin Days Destroyed VOCDD Value of Coin Days Destroyed Liveliness measures the ratio of Coin Days Destroyed to total possible Coin Days. It rises when long-term holders spend coins and falls when coins are being accumulated and held. A value close to 1 indicates high spending activity; a value close to 0 indicates strong accumulation. Sell Side Risk Ratio measures the ratio of total realized profit and loss to the realized capitalization. It indicates the degree to which investors are selling at a profit or loss relative to the overall market size.

tags.name: Crypto Marketplace
tags.description: Market capitalization in USD for the top cryptocurrencies, aggregated daily.

tags.name: Hodlers
tags.description: - **LTH Position Change 30d**: Long-Term Holder Supply Net Position Change (30-day) indicates the net change in the amount of Bitcoin accumulated or distributed by investors who hold their coins for more than 155 days. - **STH Position Change 30d**: Short-Term Holder Supply Net Position Change (30-day) indicates the net change in Bitcoin accumulated or distributed by investors who hold their coins for less than 155 days. - **LTH Position Change 7d**: Long-Term Holder Supply Net Position Change (7-day) over a 7-day period. - **STH Position Change 7d**: Short-Term Holder Supply Net Position Change (7-day) over a 7-day period. **Note**: Positive values indicate accumulation, negative values indicate distribution. - **STH/LTH Supply Ratio**: The ratio between the Bitcoin supply held by Short-Term Holders (coins moved within the last 155 days) and Long-Term Holders (coins unmoved for 155 days or more). A low ratio (STH supply much smaller than LTH) indicates strong accumulation behaviour — most of the circulating supply is in the hands of long-term investors. Historically associated with bear market bottoms and early bull market phases. A high ratio (STH supply approaching or exceeding LTH) signals increased distribution — coins are changing hands at a rapid pace, typically near cycle tops when new participants enter the market. The 155-day threshold separates STH from LTH based on on-chain UTXO age.

tags.name: Realized Profit Loss Ratio
tags.description:
It is calculated by dividing total realized profits by total realized losses over a certain period.
This metric gives insight into whether the market is more inclined to take profits or take losses, helping traders understand the current market psychology.

Here is an explanation of how to interpret it:
- Ratio > 1: More realized profits than losses. This typically suggests bullish sentiment, where more participants are selling Bitcoin at a profit.
- Ratio < 1: More realized losses than gains. This indicates bearish sentiment, where participants are selling at a loss.
- Ratio ~ 1: A neutral market, where realized profits and losses are balanced.

tags.name: BTC Issued
tags.description: ### Available metrics: BTC Issued is the daily sum of new BTC issuance per block mined. BTC Iss Cont Pct Ann is an on-chain metric that indicates what percentage the bitcoin supply grows per year due to the issuance (mining) of new BTC.

tags.name: S2F
tags.description: Stock to Flow

tags.name: UTXOs
tags.description:
UTXO count, the total number of unspent transaction outputs existing at a specified day.

 UTXOs In Profit, the number of UTXOs that are in profit.

 UTXOs In Loss, the number of UTXOs that are in loss.

UTXOs In Profit (%), the percentage of the number of UTXOs that are profitable

UTXOs In Loss (%), the percentage of the number of UTXOs that are in loss.

tags.name: MVRV
tags.description: Bitcoin Market Value Realized Value is the relationship between Market Value and Realized Value • MVRV STH (Short-Term Holder). Coins less than 155 days old.    MVRV STH > 1: Short-Term Holders are in profit. Typical area of ​​selling pressure.    MVRV STH < 1: Short-Term Holders are at a loss. Historically local capitulation zones. • MVRV LTH (Long-Term Holder). Coins with age >= 155 days.    MVRV LTH > 1: Long-Term Holders are structurally profitable.    MVRV LTH < 1: Long-Term Holders are losing money. • MVRV Z-Score provides a ratio between the current market value of Bitcoin and the price at which it was purchased (realized value) divided by volatility.    It introduces a statistical element by measuring deviations from the historical average, penalizing volatility and thus improving its analytical capabilities.

tags.name: Volatility
tags.description: Bitcoin Historical Volatility (30d & 90d), the annualized standard deviation of daily log returns over the last 30 and 90 days, expressed as a percentage. Formula: volatility = std(ln(P_t / P_{t-1}), N days) × √365 × 100 volatility30d — short-term volatility regime indicator. Spikes often coincide with sharp price moves and periods of fear or euphoria. volatility90d — medium-term structural volatility. Useful for comparing current conditions to historical cycle phases and for risk-adjusted return metrics (Sharpe, Sortino). High volatility (>80%) is typical during early bull runs and capitulation events. Low volatility (<30%) often precedes major directional moves (volatility compression).

tags.name: Investor Cap
tags.description: It is estimates the cost-basis valuation of Bitcoin holders, specifically focusing on long-term or “smart money” investors

tags.name: NVT
tags.description: NVT (Network Value to Transactions) ratio is a valuation metric for bitcoin.    It is calculated by dividing the market capitalization (network value) of BTC by its daily transaction volume.    A high NVT ratio may indicate that bitcoin is overvalued, while a low NVT ratio may suggest it is undervalued.    Investors and analysts use the NVT ratio to assess the relative value of BTC and make informed investment decisions. NVTS, the number of unique Bitcoin addresses that sent or received transactions. Only how you activate addresses that made successful transactions are taken into account.    NVT Signal = Circulating Market Cap / On-Chain Transaction Value 90 DMA

tags.name: MVRV
tags.description: Bitcoin Market Value Realized Value is the relationship between Market Value and Realized Value • MVRV STH (Short-Term Holder). Coins less than 155 days old.    MVRV STH > 1: Short-Term Holders are in profit. Typical area of ​​selling pressure.    MVRV STH < 1: Short-Term Holders are at a loss. Historically local capitulation zones. • MVRV LTH (Long-Term Holder). Coins with age >= 155 days.    MVRV LTH > 1: Long-Term Holders are structurally profitable.    MVRV LTH < 1: Long-Term Holders are losing money. • MVRV Z-Score provides a ratio between the current market value of Bitcoin and the price at which it was purchased (realized value) divided by volatility.    It introduces a statistical element by measuring deviations from the historical average, penalizing volatility and thus improving its analytical capabilities.

tags.name: Supply Shock
tags.description: ### Available metrics: Supply Shock Ratio measures the relationship between illiquid Bitcoin supply and liquid supply. It quantifies how much of the total Bitcoin supply is being held by long-term, conviction holders versus those who are more likely to sell. A rising Supply Shock Ratio indicates that more supply is being absorbed by strong holders, reducing available liquid supply and creating upward price pressure. High values suggest a supply shock environment favorable for price appreciation.

tags.name: Bitcoin Price
tags.description: • BTC Price USD • BTC Price OHLC Open, High, Low and Close. • Delta Price (%) is the price change between two specific points in time, in this case, one day and the previous day. Delta Price (USD) = Current Price − Previous Price • Sharpe Ratio measures risk-adjusted return. It is the ratio of Annualized Return to Annualized Volatility (Standard Deviation of Daily Returns multiplied by sqrt(364)). A Sharpe ratio < 0 means:    High risk    Low return    Capital is not being efficiently rewarded Historically:    Sharpe > 1 → good    Sharpe > 2 → excellent    Sharpe < 0 → hostile environment for passive strategies. • Sortino Ratio is a financial metric that measures the return on investment adjusted for downside risk (negative volatility). A Sortino ratio < 0 means:    Low return    Capital is not being efficiently rewarded Historically:    Sortino > 1 → good    Sortino > 2 → excellent    Sortino < 0 → hostile environment for passive strategies • Mayer Multiple is a technical analysis indicator that compares the current price of BTC to its 200-day moving average (MA).

tags.name: NUL
tags.description:
Net Unrealized Loss (NUL) is the sum of products of UTXO's value and the price difference between created and destroyed only in loss, divided by market_cap.
Sum of UTxO being in loss with the price difference between created and destroyed.

tags.name: NUP
tags.description:
Net Unrealized Profit (NUP) is the sum of products of UTXO's value and the price difference between created and destroyed only in profit, divided by market_cap.
Sum of UTXO being in profit with the price difference between created and destroyed.

tags.name: Market Cap
tags.description: Market capitalization data for Bitcoin (BTC). Market Cap is calculated by multiplying the current price of Bitcoin by the total number of Bitcoins in circulation.

tags.name: Bitcoin Supply
tags.description: Bitcoin Supply is the amount of BTC that exists. Supply Loss, the amount of BTC that is in loss. Supply Profit, the amount of BTC that is in profit.

tags.name: BTC Profit / Loss
tags.description: Percentage of BTC that is profitable, meaning the acquisition price is higher than today's price.

tags.name: Puell Multiple
tags.description: Bitcoin Puell Multiple is calculated by dividing the daily issuance value of bitcoins (in USD) by the 365-day moving average of the daily issuance value.

tags.name: SSR
tags.description:
Stablecoin Supply Ratio (SSR) is the ratio of Bitcoin's market cap to the total market cap of stablecoins (USDT + USDC). A lower SSR indicates more stablecoin 'dry powder' relative to BTC market cap, suggesting higher potential buying pressure. A higher SSR indicates less relative buying power from stablecoins.

SSR Oscillator normalizes the Stablecoin Supply Ratio (SSR) against its 365-day moving average (SSR / SMA365). Values above 1 indicate SSR is elevated relative to its historical average; values below 1 indicate SSR is compressed, suggesting more relative stablecoin buying power than usual.

tags.name: Stocks
tags.description: Gold, Silver, SP500, Strategy, Microsoft, Meta, Google, Apple, Amazon and Nvidia

tags.name: True Market Mean
tags.description: Bitcoin True Market Mean Price (TMMP) is the ratio of realized market capitalization to active supply.

tags.name: SOPR
tags.description: Compare the selling price of a UTXO to its value when it was acquired. SOPR = Selling price of BTC / Purchase price of the BTC spent. • SOPR STH (Short-Term Holder). Coins less than 155 days old.    SOPR STH > 1: STHs sell for profit.    SOPR STH < 1: STHs sell for loss. • SOPR LTH (Long-Term Holder). Coins with age >= 155 days.    SOPR LTH > 1: Long-Term Holders sell for profit.    SOPR LTH < 1: Long-Term Holders sell for loss. • aSOPR. The adjusted output profit ratio, is the proportion of spent outputs (with a lifespan greater than one hour) that are profitable at the time of the window.    Values ​​greater than 1 indicate that more investors are selling at a profit.    Values ​​less than 1 indicate that more investors are selling at a loss.

tags.name: Coin Days Destroyed
tags.description: ### Available metrics: Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) is the sum of the product of the number of coins spent and the number of days those coins were inactive before being spent, calculated for all coins spent on a given day. Supply Adjusted Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) is CDD value adjusted by supply. It is calculated as the value of CDD divided by total supply. Binary Coin Days Destroyed is binary value and if Supply Adjusted Coin Days Destroyed (Supply-Adjusted CDD) is larger than average Supply-Adjusted CDD, Binary Coin Days points to '1' and points to '0' if not. Supply Adjusted Dormancy is the average age of all coins spent on a given day, adjusted by supply. It is calculated as the value of Dormancy divided by total supply. Average Dormancy is the average age of all coins spent on a given day. It is calculated as the total Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) divided by the total volume of coins spent on that day. VDD Value Destroyed Days combines Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) with the price to measure how much 'old economic value' is moving on the network. VDD = (BTC × age in days) x USD Price. VDD Multiple (Value Days Destroyed Multiple) then divides a 30-day average of VDD by a 365 average to compare near-term spending velocity with a yearly average of spending velocity. MVOCDD Median Value of Coin Days Destroyed VOCDD Value of Coin Days Destroyed Liveliness measures the ratio of Coin Days Destroyed to total possible Coin Days. It rises when long-term holders spend coins and falls when coins are being accumulated and held. A value close to 1 indicates high spending activity; a value close to 0 indicates strong accumulation. Sell Side Risk Ratio measures the ratio of total realized profit and loss to the realized capitalization. It indicates the degree to which investors are selling at a profit or loss relative to the overall market size.

tags.name: Supply Illiquid
tags.description: Illiquid Supply is the amount of Bitcoin (BTC) held in addresses that have not moved their coins for at least 1 year. Liquid Supply is the complementary metric: BTC held in addresses that have moved coins within the last year. A rising Illiquid Supply indicates strong long-term holding behavior and reduced sell-side pressure. Highly Liquid Supply is the amount of Bitcoin (BTC) held in UTXOs created within the last 90 days. It represents recently moved coins that are more likely to be sold in the short term. A rising Highly Liquid Supply signals increased sell-side pressure; a falling value suggests coins are maturing into longer-term holding.

tags.name: BTC Liquidations
tags.description: Bitcoin futures liquidations occur when an exchange forcibly closes a trader's leveraged position due to insufficient margin. A SELL-side liquidation means a long position was liquidated (the trader bet on price going up); a BUY-side liquidation means a short position was liquidated (the trader bet on price going down). Large liquidation clusters are often associated with sharp price moves.

tags.name: NUPL
tags.description: Bitcoin Net Unrealized Profit Loss. It represents investor sentiment and shows what Bitcoin holders would gain or lose if they sold now. • NUPL STH (Short-Term Holder). Coins less than 155 days old. STH in unrealized gain or loss • NUPL LTH (Long-Term Holder). Coins with age >= 155 days. LTH in unrealized gain or loss • NUPL Zone, a discrete market sentiment signal derived from the Net Unrealized Profit/Loss (NUPL) ratio. NUPL = (Market Cap - Realized Cap) / Market Cap The zone classification follows the standard five-phase model: • Capitulation — NUPL < 0. The network is in aggregate loss. Historically marks cycle bottoms and extreme fear. • Fear — 0 ≤ NUPL < 0.25. Early recovery phase. Participants are cautiously optimistic. • Hope — 0.25 ≤ NUPL < 0.5. Growing confidence. Bull market gaining traction. • Optimism — 0.5 ≤ NUPL < 0.75. Strong bull market. Most holders in profit. • Euphoria — NUPL ≥ 0.75. Market overheated. Historically associated with cycle tops. The response includes both the raw nupl value and the nuplZone string label.

tags.name: SOPR
tags.description: Compare the selling price of a UTXO to its value when it was acquired. SOPR = Selling price of BTC / Purchase price of the BTC spent. • SOPR STH (Short-Term Holder). Coins less than 155 days old.    SOPR STH > 1: STHs sell for profit.    SOPR STH < 1: STHs sell for loss. • SOPR LTH (Long-Term Holder). Coins with age >= 155 days.    SOPR LTH > 1: Long-Term Holders sell for profit.    SOPR LTH < 1: Long-Term Holders sell for loss. • aSOPR the adjusted output profit ratio, is the proportion of spent outputs (with a lifespan greater than one hour) that are profitable at the time of the window.    Values ​​greater than 1 indicate that more investors are selling at a profit.    Values ​​less than 1 indicate that more investors are selling at a loss.

tags.name: Mean Coin Age
tags.description:
The Mean Coin Age indicates the average age of all BTC, weighted by the number of coins. 
The higher the Mean Coin Age, the longer the BTC has been held by owners without being spent.

tags.name: Bitcoin Dominance
tags.description: Bitcoin Dominance is a crypto market indicator that measures what percentage of the total value of all cryptocurrencies corresponds to Bitcoin.

tags.name: Supply Hot
tags.description: Hot Supply is the total amount of Bitcoin (BTC) that has been moved on-chain within the last 7 days, measured in BTC and USD. It represents the most recently active portion of the circulating supply — coins that are actively changing hands and are most likely to exert short-term selling pressure. A rising Hot Supply indicates increased on-chain activity and higher liquidity entering the market, which can signal distribution phases or heightened speculative interest. A falling Hot Supply suggests that fewer coins are being moved, consistent with accumulation and HODLing behaviour — typically associated with consolidation periods or low-volatility regimes. Hot Supply complements Highly Liquid Supply (90-day window) by providing a shorter-term view of coin velocity.

tags.name: Thermo Cap
tags.description: It is the cumulative revenue earned by Bitcoin miners.

tags.name: ASOL
tags.description: The Average Lifespan of Spent Output (ASOL) measures the average age of recently spent Bitcoin outputs. ASOL focuses exclusively on these lifetimes, without considering transaction volume, to measure the age of coins circulating on the network.

tags.name: Realized Loss
tags.description: Losses on bitcoin transactions that were bought and sold

tags.name: Addresses
tags.description: ### Available metrics: Activate Addresses, the total number of unique active addresses, inclusive of senders and receivers. Active Sender Addresses, the total number of unique active sender addresses. Active Receiver Addresses, the total number of unique active receiver addresses. NVM (Network Value to Metcalfe Ratio) is the ratio between the logarithm of the market capitalization divided by the logarithm of daily active addresses.

tags.name: Addresses Balance
tags.description: Bitcoin Balance by Address refers to the number of Bitcoin addresses that have a balance within a certain range of BTC.

tags.name: Realized Profit
tags.description: Profits on bitcoin transactions that were bought and sold

tags.name: Mempool
tags.description: Daily mempool snapshot captured at 00:20 UTC. mempoolSize is the total virtual size of all unconfirmed transactions in bytes (vbytes). mempoolFeeRate is the estimated fee rate in sat/vbyte required for confirmation in the next block (estimatesmartfee 1).

tags.name: S2F
tags.description: Stock to Flow model price

tags.name: S2F
tags.description: Stock to Flow Valuation Zone

tags.name: Exchanges
tags.description: Exchange Inflow BTC, total amount of coins transferred to the exchange. A high value indicates increased selling pressure on the spot exchange. Exchange Outflow BTC, total amount of coins transferred from the exchange. A high value indicates decreasing selling pressure in the spot exchange. Exchange Netflow BTC, the difference between coins flowing into and out of the exchange. (Inflow - Outflow = Netflow) Exchange Inflow Mean BTC, the mean amount of coins per transaction sent to the exchange. High value indicates investors are sending a greater number of coins in a transaction. It indicates higher selling pressure which could lead to the future price drop. Exchange Outflow Mean BTC, the mean amount of coins per transaction sent from the exchange. High value indicates investors are withdrawing a greater number of coins in a transaction. It indicates decreasing selling pressure. Reserve BTC, the total amount of BTC held on exchanges Exchange Reserve Trend (30d), the percentage change in the total BTC held on exchanges over the last 30 days. A negative value indicates that BTC is leaving exchanges (withdrawals exceed deposits), which is generally interpreted as a bullish signal — investors are moving coins to self-custody, reducing available selling supply. A positive value indicates that BTC is accumulating on exchanges (deposits exceed withdrawals), which is generally interpreted as a bearish signal — investors are preparing to sell. This metric helps identify sustained directional shifts in exchange reserves beyond daily noise.

tags.name: Bitcoin Supply
tags.description: Bitcoin Supply is the amount of BTC that exists. Long-Term Holder Supply (LTH Supply) is an on-chain metric that measures how much Bitcoin is held by long-term investors. LTH Supply is the total amount of BTC whose last transaction occurred more than 155 days ago. Short-Term Holder Supply (STH Supply) is an on-chain metric that measures how much Bitcoin is held by short-term investors. STH Supply is the total amount of BTC whose last transaction occurred less than 155 days ago. Supply Loss, the amount of BTC that is in loss. Supply Profit, the amount of BTC that is in profit.

tags.name: Reserve Risk
tags.description: ### Available metrics: Reserve Risk is used to assess the confidence of long-term holders relative to the price of Bitcoin at any given point in time. When confidence is high and price is low, Reserve Risk is low (attractive risk/reward to invest). When confidence is low and price is high, Reserve Risk is high (unattractive risk/reward to invest). Reserve Risk Adjusted is a normalized version of Reserve Risk that adjusts the metric to account for Bitcoin's growing adoption curve. By correcting for long-term adoption trends, it allows for more consistent comparisons across different market cycles. Like Reserve Risk, low values indicate high holder confidence relative to price (attractive risk/reward), while high values indicate low confidence relative to price (unattractive risk/reward), but with reduced bias introduced by Bitcoin's expanding user base over time.

tags.name: AVIV
tags.description: Active Value to Investor Value. The AVIV Ratio compares Bitcoin's active capitalization (i.e., the portion of BTC in circulation that is in motion—recent transactions, withdrawals from exchanges, etc.) with its realized capitalization (historical BTC value based on when it was acquired).

tags.name: Exchanges
tags.description: ### Available metrics: Exchange Inflow BTC, total amount of coins transferred to the exchange. A high value indicates increased selling pressure on the spot exchange. Exchange Outflow BTC, total amount of coins transferred from the exchange. A high value indicates decreasing selling pressure in the spot exchange. Exchange Netflow BTC, the difference between coins flowing into and out of the exchange. (Inflow - Outflow = Netflow) Exchange Inflow Mean BTC, the mean amount of coins per transaction sent to the exchange. High value indicates investors are sending a greater number of coins in a transaction. It indicates higher selling pressure which could lead to the future price drop. Exchange Outflow Mean BTC, the mean amount of coins per transaction sent from the exchange. High value indicates investors are withdrawing a greater number of coins in a transaction. It indicates decreasing selling pressure. Reserve BTC, the total amount of BTC held on exchanges

tags.name: Realized Cap
tags.description: Cumulative valuation of all bitcoins at the price they were last moved, providing a more accurate representation of market value than traditional market cap.

tags.name: Investor Price
tags.description: Export Investor Price data as CSV.

tags.name: Hodlers
tags.description: STH/LTH Supply Ratio, the ratio between the Bitcoin supply held by Short-Term Holders (coins moved within the last 155 days) and Long-Term Holders (coins unmoved for 155 days or more). A low ratio (STH supply much smaller than LTH) indicates strong accumulation behaviour — most of the circulating supply is in the hands of long-term investors. Historically associated with bear market bottoms and early bull market phases. A high ratio (STH supply approaching or exceeding LTH) signals increased distribution — coins are changing hands at a rapid pace, typically near cycle tops when new participants enter the market. The 155-day threshold separates STH from LTH based on on-chain UTXO age.
